edges.description: Without an assembled central element, homologous chromosomes cannot synapse during meiotic prophase I.
Unsynapsed chromosomes trigger the pachytene checkpoint, arresting germ-cell meiotic progression.
edges.target: Meiotic prophase I pachytene checkpoint activation

edges.description: Pachytene-arrested germ cells are eliminated, depleting the ovarian follicle pool in 46,XX individuals and the spermatogenic lineage in 46,XY individuals.
edges.source: Meiotic prophase I pachytene checkpoint activation
edges.target: Germ-cell depletion and sex-dimorphic gonadal failure

edges.description: In 46,XX individuals follicle depletion presents clinically as primary ovarian insufficiency.
edges.source: Germ-cell depletion and sex-dimorphic gonadal failure

edges.description: In 46,XY individuals germ-cell depletion presents clinically as non-obstructive azoospermia.
edges.source: Germ-cell depletion and sex-dimorphic gonadal failure

nodes.description: The meiotic block depletes germ cells in both sexes, producing ovarian follicle depletion with primary ovarian insufficiency in 46,XX individuals and germ-cell-depleted testes with non-obstructive azoospermia in 46,XY individuals. The same molecular lesion thus yields a sex-dimorphic clinical presentation.
nodes.id: Germ-cell depletion and sex-dimorphic gonadal failure

evidence.reference_title: Variations of C14ORF39 and SYCE1 Identified in Idiopathic Premature Ovarian Insufficiency and Nonobstructive Azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: Our study identified novel pathogenic variations of C14ORF39 and SYCE1 in sporadic patients with POI or NOA, highlighting the essential role of SC genes in the maintenance of ovarian and testicular function.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing reveals SYCE1 mutation associated with autosomal recessive primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: These results highlight the importance of the synaptonemal complex and meiosis in ovarian function.

nodes.description: SYCE1 forms the central element of the synaptonemal complex and is required for physical synapsis of homologous chromosomes during meiotic prophase I. Its loss prevents stable synapsis of homologous chromosome pairs, leaving them unsynapsed at the pachytene stage.

evidence.reference_title: Familial primary ovarian insufficiency associated with an SYCE1 point mutation: defective meiosis elucidated in humanized mice.
evidence.snippet: Our results strongly support a causative role of this mutation for the POI phenotype in human patients, and the mechanisms involved would relate to defects in homologous chromosome synapsis.

nodes.description: Unsynapsed chromosomes or persistent recombination intermediates from failed synapsis activate the meiotic pachytene checkpoint, which surveys for completion of synapsis and crossover formation. Checkpoint activation arrests germ cells at the pachytene stage of prophase I; persistently arrested cells are eliminated by apoptosis, depleting the germ-cell pool.
nodes.id: Meiotic prophase I pachytene checkpoint activation

evidence.reference_title: Novel copy number variations within SYCE1 caused meiotic arrest and non-obstructive azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: H&E and IF staining demonstrated that the spermatogenesis was arrested at pachytene stage in the two patients with NOA, suggesting these two novel CNVs within SYCE1 could lead to meiotic defect and NOA.

nodes.description: In 46,XY individuals SYCE1 deficiency causes non-obstructive azoospermia due to pachytene-stage meiotic arrest of spermatogenesis.
Reported 46,XX individuals present with primary ovarian insufficiency, ranging from absent pubertal maturation to early ovarian failure.

evidence.reference_title: Familial primary ovarian insufficiency associated with an SYCE1 point mutation: defective meiosis elucidated in humanized mice.
evidence.snippet: Our results strongly support a causative role of this mutation for the POI phenotype in human patients, and the mechanisms involved would relate to defects in homologous chromosome synapsis.

evidence.reference_title: Exome sequencing reveals SYCE1 mutation associated with autosomal recessive primary ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: We describe for the first time a homozygous mutation in SYCE1 in humans.

evidence.reference_title: Novel copy number variations within SYCE1 caused meiotic arrest and non-obstructive azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: We identified that two novel CNVs within SYCE1 are associated with meiotic arrest and male infertility.

nodes.description: SYCE1 encodes a central element protein of the synaptonemal complex (SC). Biallelic loss-of-function abolishes SYCE1 protein and disrupts its interactions with the transverse-filament protein SYCP1 and the central element partner SIX6OS1/C14ORF39, so the SC central element cannot assemble and homologous chromosomes fail to synapse during meiotic prophase I.

evidence.reference_title: Variations of C14ORF39 and SYCE1 Identified in Idiopathic Premature Ovarian Insufficiency and Nonobstructive Azoospermia.
evidence.snippet: the variations in SYCE1 disrupted its interaction with SYCP1 or C14ORF39, both of which affected SC assembly and meiosis.

evidence.reference_title: Familial primary ovarian insufficiency associated with an SYCE1 point mutation: defective meiosis elucidated in humanized mice.
evidence.snippet: No SYCE1 protein was detected in homozygous mutants and Syce1 transcript level was highly diminished, suggesting transcript degradation as the basis of the infertility mechanism.
